---
title: "See, Hear, and Read: Multimodal AI Made Simple"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, solarized]
    slide-number: true
    footer: "Multimodal AI ≠ scary — it’s practical 🚀"
    transition: fade
    incremental: true
    chalkboard: true
---

# What is “multimodal”?

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Definition
**Multimodal AI** refers to models that can **work with and integrate multiple types of data**, such as **text, images, audio, and video**.

- Understands more than one “sense”
- Connects signals (e.g., audio ↔ text)
- Powers practical workflows (not just research)
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
### Daily examples
- 🎧 **Captions** on videos (audio ➝ text)
- 🔎 **Google Lens**: point camera to translate (image ➝ text)
- 🗣️ **Voice assistants** reading messages (text ➝ audio)
- 📸 **Receipt scanners**/document apps (image ➝ searchable text)
- 🖼️ **Alt text** generation for accessibility (image ➝ text)
- 🚘 **Driver alerts** reading notifications aloud (text ➝ audio)
:::
 
::::

::: notes
Keep it simple:
- Emphasize “multiple senses” idea.
- Tie to audience’s daily tools so it feels familiar.
- Promise: “You can build these with Python in a few lines.”
:::
